title: Front-half ecosystem note deepening phase
summary: The front-half note layer now needs a small zone-faithful cleanup more than raw note count. This wave adds one new beach note for `sanderling`, one new coastal-scrub note for `beach-pea`, and revises the most obvious beach and scrub note mismatches so the journal teaches relationships that are actually visible in the named zone.

execution_order.goal: Prepare front-half ecosystem note deepening handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Ecosystem notes are the main teaching surface; the front half needs more.
More notes means deeper understanding of beach and coastal ecology.
Notes must teach real ecology, not repeat fact text.

guardrails: Notes must teach observable habitat relationships, not abstract concepts.
Each note should deepen understanding of entry neighbors, not repeat individual facts.
Notes should tie to zones where the relationship is actually visible.
Observation prompts attached to new notes should read like notebook questions.
Because note resolution returns the first unlocked matching note, new notes should favor entries without a current local note payoff instead of overloading existing anchors.
Preserve note ids already referenced by live field-partner or field-request seams.
